title: DDL Commands Example
description: Examples of Data Definition Language commands used to define database structure
explanation: DDL commands define the database structure (schema). CREATE creates objects, ALTER modifies them, DROP deletes them. These commands automatically commit and cannot be rolled back.

title: DML Commands Example
description: Examples of Data Manipulation Language commands for inserting, updating, and deleting data
code:
-- DML Commands Example

-- Insert data into table
INSERT INTO Student (StudentID, Name, Age, Course)
VALUES (1, 'John', 20, 'Computer Science');

-- Update existing data
UPDATE Student
SET Age = 21
WHERE StudentID = 1;

-- Delete data
DELETE FROM Student
WHERE StudentID = 1;

-- Query data (most common DML)
SELECT Name, Course
FROM Student
WHERE Age > 18;
explanation: DML commands manipulate data within tables. These operations can be controlled using transactions (COMMIT/ROLLBACK) for maintaining data integrity.

complexity.time: O(n) for large tables without indexes

title: DCL Commands Example
description: Examples of Data Control Language commands for security and access management
explanation: DCL controls access to database objects. TCL manages transactions - COMMIT saves changes permanently, ROLLBACK undoes uncommitted changes.
